specs.key_requirements: Existing Phase 1+2 entry points: spec_alignment::check(path) for format+logical rules, spec_alignment::check_with_coverage(spec_dir, source_dirs) for full coverage analysis. CheckResult schema: { files, total_violations, passed, coverage: Option<CoverageReport> }. Phase 3 consumers call check() for format validation and check_with_coverage() for coverage warnings. ViolationKind enum covers all violation types surfaced in workflow responses.

sdd_run_change result schema currently has: change_id, action, current_phase, executor, message, next_actions — needs alignment_warnings field added carrying full violation list as complete JSON. sdd_workflow_create_change_merge response schema: currently has specs_merged, archive_path, audit_log — alignment_warnings added here too.

sdd_artifact_create_change_spec must run spec_alignment::check() on the ENTIRE spec file after writing any section. Implementation note: tool returns filled_sections, status, next_actions in practice (from Rust source); spec will need to add formal result schema with alignment_violations array. Format violations (Phase 1) block the write; coverage gaps (Phase 2) are warnings only.

After specs are merged to cclab/specs/, run check() on each merged spec and append alignment warnings to implementation.md review section as ## Alignment Warnings subsection. Insert between Write and Archive steps. Non-fatal: alignment violations should NOT block the merge — they are warnings only. implementation.md format: inline ## Review: {spec_id} sections — alignment warnings appended there.

Phase routing table: ChangeSpec* phases → sdd_workflow_create_change_spec; ChangeMerge* → sdd_workflow_create_change_merge. sdd_run_change is the outer bridge — alignment_warnings added to its response. State transitions do not change for Phase 3 — alignment check is a non-blocking side effect.

Section fill lifecycle: skeleton → fill sections via sdd_artifact_create_change_spec → prune → review — check runs after each fill call on the full spec file. Artifact writing enforcement: agents must use CLI artifact tools — the post-write check runs inside the tool.
implementation.md inline review format: ## Review: {spec_id} sections with Verdict, Summary, Checklist. Alignment warnings appended to the review section of implementation.md during merge.

Skill loop calls sdd_run_change repeatedly — alignment_warnings in response will be visible to mainthread. Skill template update to display alignment_warnings to user is out-of-scope for Phase 3 (passive field only; display behavior is a future skill enhancement).
